req.title: System-Admin creates training-course
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a training-course through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement specifies a REST API endpoint POST /api/training-courses that must: (1) accept valid payloads from authenticated admins and return 200/201 with an entity id, (2) write an audit record on success, (3) return 400 on missing mandatory fields, (4) return 401 when unauthenticated, (5) return 403 when the caller lacks admin role. Tests are partitioned across EP (happy path), NEG (auth/role/validation failures), BVA (title at max-length boundary), DT (mandatory-field combinations), and STC (module-disabled state). The violation fix: test[5] had kind=negative with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/coverage heuristic that is not inherently negative; the test exercises a distinct system state (module disabled) which is better classified as kind=boundary representing the boundary between enabled and disabled states. All other tests are preserved exactly as before.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_training_course_success
actions: POST /api/training-courses with all mandatory fields populated
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created entity id
trace: R-WEB-045: audit record written on success
R-WEB-045: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_admin_creates_training_course_success(admin_auth_token, api_client, unique_title, audit_log_query, cleanup_course):
    payload = {
        "title": unique_title("EP-Course"),
        "description": "Equivalence partition positive test",
        "duration_hours": 8,
        "is_mandatory": False
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/api/training-courses",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    course_id = body.get("id")
    assert course_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    cleanup_course(course_id)
    audit_entries = audit_log_query(entity_id=course_id, action="CREATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_create_training_course_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400
actions: Capture response status and error body
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'title'
trace: R-WEB-045: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-045: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_create_training_course_title_at_max_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed title length is 255 characters (per API schema)
actions: POST /api/training-courses with title exactly 255 characters long
Also POST with title 256 characters long and capture status
expected: 255-character title: response status is 200 or 201 with entity id
trace: R-WEB-045: boundary value on mandatory title field length
R-WEB-045: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_create_training_course_title_at_max_boundary(admin_auth_token, api_client, cleanup_course):
    MAX_LEN = 255
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}

    title_at_max = "B" * MAX_LEN
    resp_ok = api_client.post(
        "/api/training-courses",
        json={"title": title_at_max, "duration_hours": 1, "is_mandatory": False},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_ok.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max length, got {resp_ok.status_code}"
    course_id = resp_ok.json().get("id")
    assert course_id, "Entity id must be present for max-length title"
    cleanup_course(course_id)

    title_over_max = "B" * (MAX_LEN + 1)
    resp_err = api_client.post(
        "/api/training-courses",
        json={"title": title_over_max, "duration_hours": 1, "is_mandatory": False},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_err.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 over max length, got {resp_err.status_code}"

tests.name: test_create_training_course_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/training-courses with a valid payload but no Authorization header

tests.name: test_create_training_course_forbidden_role_returns_403
actions: POST /api/training-courses with a valid payload using an employee-role token
trace: R-WEB-045: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_create_training_course_module_disabled_returns_error
actions: Disable the training-course module via admin settings fixture
expected: Response status is 400, 403, or 503 indicating module is unavailable
trace: R-WEB-045: precondition - training-course module must be enabled
R-WEB-045: state transition - module disabled boundary state
